directive.title: Dashboard load performance budget assertion and instrumentation

auditor.summary: The implementation successfully creates all required files and satisfies 15 out of 18 acceptance criteria. The core functionality (timing utility, Server-Timing headers, client-side performance monitoring, and CI budget check) is correctly implemented. However, there are two HIGH-severity issues: (1) the dashboard page uses [key: string]: any which violates TypeScript strict mode and the directive's explicit constraint against 'any' types, and (2) this will cause tsc --noEmit to fail. Additionally, there are MEDIUM issues with potential ESLint errors from unused imports and code organization. The build is recoverable with minor fixes but currently fails TypeScript compilation requirements.

top_issues.description: Button component uses [key: string]: any which violates TypeScript strict mode and the directive's constraint against 'any' types without explicit justification

useState is imported but the component uses it directly (setRfqData, setLoading, setError) - this is actually fine, but the import of useState is unused in the sense that it's used. However, the component also imports React types that may cause ESLint unused variable warnings

The component defines inline UI components (Card, CardHeader, CardContent, Button, Badge) which are not exported and may cause duplication if used elsewhere. This is not a bug but violates DRY principles
